snp3.7:1.2: that on one occasion, while the Blessed One was wandering on a tour among the Aṅguttarāpans with a large Saṅgha of monks, approximately 1,250 monks in all, he arrived at an Aṅguttarāpan town named Āpaṇa.
snp3.7:1.3: Keṇiya the coiled hair ascetic heard it said,
snp3.7:1.4: “Gotama the contemplative—a son of the Sakyans, gone forth from the Sakyan clan—on a wandering tour among the Aṅguttarāpans with a large Saṅgha of monks, approximately 1,250 monks in all—has arrived at Āpaṇa.
snp3.7:1.5: And of that Master Gotama this fine reputation has spread:
snp3.7:1.6: ‘He is indeed a Blessed One, worthy & rightly self-awakened, consummate in clear-knowing & conduct, well-gone, an expert with regard to the cosmos, unexcelled trainer of people fit to be tamed, teacher of devas & human beings, awakened, blessed.’
snp3.7:1.7: He makes known—having realized it through direct knowledge—this world with its devas, Māras, & Brahmās, this generation with its contemplatives & brahmans, its rulers & commonfolk;
snp3.7:1.8: he explains the Dhamma admirable in the beginning, admirable in the middle, admirable in the end; he expounds the holy life both in its particulars & in its essence, entirely perfect, surpassingly pure.
snp3.7:1.9: It is good to see such a worthy one.’”
snp3.7:2.1: So Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, exchanged courteous greetings with him.
snp3.7:2.2: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side.
snp3.7:2.3: As he was sitting there, the Blessed One instructed, urged, roused, & encouraged him with a talk on Dhamma.
snp3.7:2.4: Then Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic—instructed, urged, roused, & encouraged with the Blessed One’s talk on Dhamma—said to him,
snp3.7:2.5: “Master Gotama, may the Blessed One acquiesce to my meal tomorrow, together with the Saṅgha of monks.”
snp3.7:2.6: When this was said, the Blessed One said to him,
snp3.7:2.7: “The Saṅgha of monks is large, Keṇiya, approximately 1,250 monks in all, and you are strongly confident in brahmans.”
snp3.7:4.1: A third time, Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic said to the Blessed One,
snp3.7:4.2: “Master Gotama, even though the Saṅgha of monks is large, approximately 1,250 monks in all, and I am strongly confident in brahmans,
snp3.7:4.3: may Master Gotama acquiesce to my meal tomorrow, together with the Saṅgha of monks.”
snp3.7:4.4: The Blessed One acquiesced with silence.
snp3.7:4.5: Then Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic, understanding the Blessed One’s acquiescence, got up from his seat and returned to his own ashram. On arrival, he addressed his friends & companions, his relatives & kinsmen:
snp3.7:4.6: “Listen to me, venerable friends & companions, relatives & kinsmen:
snp3.7:4.7: I have invited Gotama the contemplative together with a Saṅgha of monks, approximately 1,250 monks in all, for the meal tomorrow.
snp3.7:4.8: Give me bodily assistance for that.”
snp3.7:4.9: “As you say, master,” his friends & companions, relatives & kinsmen responded to him. Some of them dug out ovens, some split wood, some washed dishes, some set out a water pot, some arranged seats,
snp3.7:4.10: while Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic arranged a pavilion of garlands himself.
snp3.7:5.1: Now on that occasion Sela the brahman was staying at Āpaṇa. He was a master of the Three Vedas with their vocabularies, liturgy, phonology, etymology, & histories as a fifth; skilled in philology & grammar, he was fully versed in cosmology and in the marks of a Great Man. He was instructing 300 young brahmans in the (Vedic) mantras. Then, as he—surrounded by 300 young brahmans—was walking and wandering about to exercise his legs,
snp3.7:6.1: he arrived at the ashram of Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic.
snp3.7:6.2: He saw that some of the people in the ashram were digging out ovens, some were splitting wood…
snp3.7:6.3: while Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic was arranging a pavilion of garlands himself.
snp3.7:6.4: On seeing this, he said to Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic,
snp3.7:6.5: “Could it be that Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic is giving a son or daughter in marriage, or is he setting up a great sacrifice, or is Seniya Bimbisāra, the king of Magadha, invited for tomorrow together with his army?”
snp3.7:7.1: “No, Sela, it’s not that I’m giving a son or daughter in marriage, nor that Seniya Bimbisāra, the king of Magadha, is invited for tomorrow together with his army.
snp3.7:7.2: But I am setting up a great sacrifice.
snp3.7:7.3: There is Gotama the contemplative—a son of the Sakyans, gone forth from the Sakyan clan—who, on a wandering tour among the Aṅguttarāpans with a large Saṅgha of monks, approximately 1,250 monks in all, has arrived at Āpaṇa.
snp3.7:7.4: And of that Master Gotama this fine reputation has spread: ‘He is indeed a Blessed One, worthy & rightly self-awakened, consummate in clear-knowing & conduct, well-gone, an expert with regard to the cosmos, unexcelled trainer of people fit to be tamed, teacher of devas & human beings,
snp3.7:7.6: He has been invited by me for the meal tomorrow, together with the Saṅgha of monks.”
snp3.7:8.1: Then the thought occurred to Sela the brahman,
snp3.7:8.2: “Even just this word is hard to come by in the world: ‘Awakened.’
snp3.7:8.3: Now, these 32 marks of a Great Man have come down in our mantras. For a Great Man endowed with them, two future courses, and no other, exist.
snp3.7:8.4: If he inhabits a home, he will become a wheel-turning king, righteous, a king through righteousness, a conqueror of the four quarters, a stabilizer of his countryside endowed with the seven treasures.
snp3.7:8.6: the wheel-treasure, the elephant-treasure, the horse-treasure, the jewel-treasure, the woman-treasure, the householder-treasure, and the adviser-treasure as the seventh.
snp3.7:8.7: He will have a thousand sons, valiant, heroic in body, crushers of enemy armies.
snp3.7:8.8: He will dwell, having conquered the earth to the edge of the sea, by means of Dhamma, without rod, without sword.
snp3.7:8.9: But if he goes forth from the home life into homelessness, he will become a worthy one, rightly self-awakened, with his roof opened in the world.”
snp3.7:8.10: “Master Keṇiya, where is he staying now, that Master Gotama, worthy & rightly self-awakened?”
snp3.7:9.1: When this was said, Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic lifted his right arm and said to Sela the brahman,
snp3.7:9.2: “There, by the dark green line of the forest.”
snp3.7:9.3: Then Sela the brahman, together with the 300 young brahmans, headed toward the Blessed One.
snp3.7:9.4: Then he addressed the young brahmans,
snp3.7:9.5: “Come noiselessly, masters, placing a footstep in a footstep.
snp3.7:9.6: These Blessed Ones are hard to approach, like a lion wandering alone.
snp3.7:9.7: And when I engage Gotama the contemplative in counsel, don’t break into the middle of my talk.
snp3.7:9.8: Wait until the end of my talk.”
snp3.7:10.1: Then Sela the brahman went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, exchanged courteous greetings with him.
snp3.7:10.2: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side.
snp3.7:10.3: As he was sitting there, he looked for the 32 marks of a Great Man in the Blessed One’s body.
snp3.7:10.4: He saw most of the 32 marks of a Great Man in the Blessed One’s body, except for two.
snp3.7:10.5: About the two marks he was doubtful, uncertain, undecided, and wasn’t reassured:
snp3.7:10.6: about the male organ being in a sheath, and about the size of the tongue.
snp3.7:11.1: The thought occurred to the Blessed One,
snp3.7:11.2: “Sela the brahman sees most of my marks of a Great Man, except for two.
snp3.7:11.3: About the two marks he is doubtful, uncertain, undecided, and isn’t reassured:
snp3.7:11.4: about the male organ being in a sheath, and about the size of the tongue.”
snp3.7:11.5: So the Blessed One willed a willing of power such that Sela the brahman saw that the Blessed One’s male organ was in a sheath.
snp3.7:11.6: And then, extending his tongue, the Blessed One licked both ear-holes and nostrils back and forth, and covered his whole forehead with his tongue.
snp3.7:12.1: The thought occurred to Sela the brahman,
snp3.7:12.2: “Gotama the contemplative’s 32 marks of a Great Man are complete and not incomplete.
snp3.7:12.3: But I don’t know whether he is awakened or not.
snp3.7:12.4: Still, I have heard the aged line of teachers, along in their years, say that
snp3.7:12.5: Blessed Ones, worthy & rightly self-awakened, reveal themselves when their praise is spoken.
snp3.7:12.6: What if I were to praise Gotama the contemplative face-to-face with fitting verses?”
snp3.7:12.7: So Sela the brahman praised the Blessed One face-to-face with fitting verses:
snp3.7:13.3: Blessed One, you have a golden complexion,
snp3.7:13.4: you have white teeth, you’re vigorous.
snp3.7:14.1: The characteristics of a well-born man,
snp3.7:14.2: the marks of a Great Man,
snp3.7:14.3: are all present in your body.
snp3.7:15.1: Clear your eyes, good your face,
snp3.7:15.3: In the midst of a community of contemplatives
snp3.7:16.1: A monk with skin resembling gold is admirable to look at,
snp3.7:16.3: but what use is the contemplative state for you,
snp3.7:17.1: You deserve to be a king,
snp3.7:17.4: sovereign lord of the Jambu grove.
snp3.7:18.3: King of kings, monarch of human beings:
snp3.7:19.4: I turn the wheel with Dhamma—
snp3.7:19.5: the wheel whose turning can’t be stopped.”
snp3.7:20.4: But who, master, is your general,
snp3.7:20.5: the disciple right below the teacher?
snp3.7:23.1: What should be directly known has been directly known,
snp3.7:24.3: It’s hard often to gain sight
snp3.7:25.1: I am one whose appearance in the world is hard often to gain:
snp3.7:26.4: I rejoice, with no fear from anywhere.”
snp3.7:27.2: how he speaks, this One with Eyes,
snp3.7:27.4: He roars like a lion in the forest.
snp3.7:28.3: Who, even one of a dark birth, on seeing him,
snp3.7:29.1: Whoever wants to may follow me.
snp3.7:29.4: in the presence of the one of foremost discernment.”
snp3.7:30.1: Sela’s following: “If, master, you thus delight
snp3.7:30.2: in the message of the Rightly Self-Awakened One,
snp3.7:30.3: we, too, will here go forth
snp3.7:30.4: in the presence of the one of foremost discernment.”
snp3.7:31.2: request, with hands palm-to-palm over the heart:
snp3.7:32.4: where the Going-forth is not in vain
snp3.7:32.5: for a heedful person who trains.”
snp3.7:33.1: Then Sela the brahman, together with his following, obtained the Going-forth in the Blessed One’s presence, he obtained Acceptance.
snp3.7:33.2: Then, as the night was ending, Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic, having had choice staple & non-staple foods prepared in his own ashram, announced the time to the Blessed One,
snp3.7:33.3: “It is time, master Gotama. The meal is ready.”
snp3.7:33.4: Then, early in the morning, the Blessed One—after adjusting his lower robe and carrying his bowl & outer robe—went together with a Saṅgha of monks to Keṇiya’s ashram. On arrival, he sat down on an arranged seat.
snp3.7:34.1: Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic, with his own hand, served & satisfied the Blessed One & the Saṅgha of monks with choice staple & non-staple foods.
snp3.7:34.2: Then, when the Blessed One had eaten and had rinsed his bowl & hands, Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic took a lower seat and sat to one side.
snp3.7:34.3: As he was sitting there, the Blessed One expressed approval of him with these verses:
snp3.7:35.1: “The fire oblation is the chief of sacrifices,
snp3.7:35.2: the Sāvitti, the chief of Vedic hymns,
snp3.7:35.3: a king, chief among human beings,
snp3.7:36.1: the moon, chief of the zodiac stars,
snp3.7:36.2: the sun, chief of things that burn,
snp3.7:36.3: and for those who sacrifice, wishing for merit,
snp3.7:37.1: Then, having expressed approval of Keṇiya the coiled-hair ascetic with these verses, the Blessed One got up from his seat and left.
snp3.7:37.2: Then Ven. Sela, together with his following—dwelling alone, secluded, heedful, ardent, & resolute—in no long time reached & remained in the supreme goal of the holy life, for which clansmen rightly go forth from home into homelessness, knowing & realizing it for himself in the here-&-now. He knew: “Birth is ended, the holy life fulfilled, the task done. There is nothing further for the sake of this world.”
snp3.7:37.3: And so Ven. Sela, together with his following, became another one of the arahants.
snp3.7:38.1: Then Ven. Sela, together with his following, went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, arranging his upper robe over one shoulder and extending his hands palm-to-palm over the heart toward the Blessed One, addressed him in verse:
snp3.7:39.1: “This is the eighth day since coming for refuge, One with Eyes.
snp3.7:39.4: we have tamed ourselves with your message.
snp3.7:40.2: You: Sage who has conquered Māra.
snp3.7:40.4: having crossed over, bring this generation across.
snp3.7:41.3: you are a lion free of clinging,
snp3.7:42.2: stand with hands palm-to-palm over the heart.
snp3.7:42.4: The nāgas venerate them, the feet of the Teacher.”
